description: Iran's Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Vezarat-e Omur-e Khareje) is the executive body responsible for conducting the country's foreign policy, managing diplomatic missions, and negotiating international agreements. In practice, strategic foreign policy is set by the [Office of the Supreme Leader](/orgs/office-of-the-supreme-leader) and coordinated through the [Supreme National Security Council](/orgs/supreme-national-security-council), with the Ministry implementing day-to-day diplomacy. Foreign Ministers since 1981: [Ali Akbar Velayati](/people/ali-akbar-velayati) (1981–1997), [Kamal Kharrazi](/people/kamal-kharrazi) (1997–2005), [Manouchehr Mottaki](/people/manouchehr-mottaki) (2005–2010), [Ali Akbar Salehi](/people/ali-akbar-salehi) (2010–2013), [Mohammad Javad Zarif](/people/javad-zarif) (2013–2021), [Hossein Amir-Abdollahian](/people/hossein-amir-abdollahian) (2021–2024), [Abbas Araghchi](/people/abbas-araghchi) (2024–present).
editorial_notes: The FM must be approved by the Supreme Leader per the constitution. Zarif's appointment was reportedly cleared by Khamenei directly in 2013.
